message:
feat(monitor,scanner): stale daemon detection, early ballast release, recursive inode scanning, and predictive target floor

Four improvements to the storage ballast monitoring pipeline:

1. cli_app.rs — Stale daemon detection (I26): The `status` command now
   checks the state file's modification time against a threshold derived
   from the configured poll interval (2x poll_interval_ms). This prevents
   reporting a crashed daemon as "running" when the state file exists but
   hasn't been updated recently.

2. loop_main.rs — Early ballast release at Yellow/Orange pressure: The
   daemon previously only released ballast files at Red pressure level.
   Now Yellow triggers conditional ballast release (via maybe_release)
   and Orange triggers unconditional ballast release before scanning,
   giving the system more headroom before hitting critical thresholds.
   Also wires the prediction action_horizon_minutes config into the
   PID pressure controller when predictive mode is enabled.

3. monitor/ewma.rs — Added sample_count field to RateEstimate: The
   EWMA rate estimator now exposes how many samples it has collected,
   enabling downstream consumers (like the predictive policy) to gate
   decisions on statistical significance without maintaining separate
   counters.

4. monitor/predictive.rs — Policy-driven target floor: The preemptive
   cleanup recommendation now uses a fixed policy floor (15%..25%)
   interpolated by urgency progress instead of collapsing
   recommended_free_target_pct to the current (potentially very low)
   free percentage. Added test confirming the floor holds even at 4%
   free space.

5. scanner/deletion.rs — Recursive inode scanning with cycle protection:
   collect_inode_set now walks the full directory tree (not just direct
   children) to detect open files in nested subdirectories, with:
   - MAX_INODE_SCAN_ENTRIES (20K) cap to bound memory and time
   - visited_dirs set keyed on (dev, ino) to prevent symlink/hardlink cycles
   - Added test (nested_open_file_is_detected_for_parent_directory)
     proving that an open file 3 levels deep marks the parent as in-use

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -21,6 +21,7 @@ pub struct RateEstimate {
     pub acceleration: f64,
     pub seconds_to_exhaustion: f64,
     pub seconds_to_threshold: f64,
+    pub sample_count: u64,
     pub confidence: f64,
     pub trend: Trend,
     pub alpha_used: f64,
@@ -126,6 +127,7 @@ impl DiskRateEstimator {
             acceleration: self.ewma_accel,
             seconds_to_exhaustion,
             seconds_to_threshold,
+            sample_count: self.samples,
             confidence,
             trend,
             alpha_used: alpha,
@@ -169,6 +171,7 @@ impl DiskRateEstimator {
             } else {
                 f64::INFINITY
             },
+            sample_count: self.samples,
             confidence: self.compute_confidence(),
             trend: classify_trend(self.ewma_rate, self.ewma_accel),
             alpha_used: self.base_alpha,
